prevalence.trend: increasing with aging population; vaccination programs expanding

incidence.source: European zoster surveillance and cohort studies

burden.dalyPerCapita: low overall; post-herpetic neuralgia drives disability
burden.source: WHO Europe/IHME neurologic pain burden context

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Typical dermatomal vesicular rash with neuropathic pain; PCR for atypical cases; urgent ophthalmology evaluation for ocular involvement

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Millions of lifetime cases; hundreds of thousands annually
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with early antivirals and vaccination in eligible groups

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care diagnosis with rapid referral for complications
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Antivirals within 72 hours, analgesia and neuropathic pain therapy; recombinant zoster vaccine available in many countries
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Variation in vaccine reimbursement and uptake across countries
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong access to antivirals, pain management and vaccination programs
